story: The door opens and a man walks in. “Hello, Charles,” he says, sitting by my hospital bed but refusing my hand. He claims he has a cold. I laugh; I’m dying. Days ago I had a tiny revolver smuggled in and kept it under my pillow for whatever might come. He folds his arms and says I chose this life and will be judged. I say I know who sent him. He glances at the door when I ask if his partner waits outside. I smile and slide my hand beneath the pillow. “You’re wrong,” I say.
implausible_knowledge: Days ago I had a tiny revolver smuggled in and kept it under my pillow for whatever might come
question: Seeing Charles slide his hand under the pillow and challenge him, what will the man most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Lunge to pin Charles’s wrist to stop him drawing the gun.
answer_without_knowledge: Step closer to the bed, reaching toward Charles.
